_meta.description: Curated IPC (1860) -> BNS (Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023) section correspondence for the offences citizens most commonly ask about.
_meta.transition_rule: Offences committed on or after 2024-07-01 are charged under the BNS. Offences before that date remain under the IPC. Use this to present the correct current reference while still answering about historic matters.
_meta.source: Compiled from publicly published IPC<->BNS correspondence tables (MHA / legislative summaries).
_meta.verification_note: VERIFY every entry against the official Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 bare act before relying on it. Subsection numbers in particular should be confirmed.

34.offence: Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention

124A.note: Reframed in BNS as acts endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India; not a one-to-one re-enactment.

153A.offence: Promoting enmity between groups on grounds of religion, race, etc.

295A.offence: Deliberate acts to outrage religious feelings

304.offence: Culpable homicide not amounting to murder

309.note: Not retained as a general offence (see Mental Healthcare Act, 2017). BNS s.226 covers attempt to suicide to compel or restrain a public servant.

324.offence: Voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means

326.offence: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means

326A.offence: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by use of acid

326B.offence: Voluntarily throwing or attempting to throw acid

354.offence: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty

354B.offence: Assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to disrobe

366.offence: Kidnapping or abducting a woman to compel marriage, etc.

377.note: No direct equivalent retained in the BNS.

380.offence: Theft in a dwelling house, etc.

420.offence: Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property

468.offence: Forgery for the purpose of cheating

471.offence: Using as genuine a forged document

498A.offence: Cruelty by husband or his relatives

504.offence: Intentional insult to provoke breach of peace

509.offence: Word, gesture or act intended to insult the modesty of a woman

69.offence: Sexual intercourse by employing deceitful means or false promise of marriage
